mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal loses predictive power with p > 0.1 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, which accelerates corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive returns when either series reverts to negative growth, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts short-term equity returns during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion regimes, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month inflation reading above 0.5% triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the inflation threshold shows no predictive edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm sustainable labor market tightening, which supports consumer confidence and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal loses predictive accuracy when payroll growth is driven by temporary government hiring, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate economic activity, which increases corporate profit expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening business investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in > 55% of instances when INDPRO drops below the threshold, or the signal fails to maintain statistical significance in out-of-sample periods.
